steps.title: Identify what 'and' is connecting
steps.content: The sentence contains “determine” followed by two objects: “how current-driven mixing varies across regions” and “what impact it has on the distribution of heat and nutrients in the ocean.” The conjunction “and” joins these two parallel noun clauses.

steps.title: Match the punctuation rule to 'and' joining parallel elements
steps.content: When “and” joins parallel noun clauses or phrases, no punctuation precedes it. A comma (A), colon (B), or semicolon (C) would incorrectly separate parallel items that should flow together. That matches choice D — D is correct.
